techniques.name: Direct Syscall
techniques.description: 通过直接系统调用绕过用户态Hook，避免EDR检测

techniques.name: Indirect Syscall
techniques.description: 通过间接系统调用，跳转到NTDLL中的syscall指令地址，更隐蔽

techniques.name: API Hashing
techniques.description: 通过哈希值动态解析API地址，隐藏导入表

techniques.name: String XOR Encryption
techniques.description: XOR加密字符串，避免静态分析检测

techniques.name: Permission Flipping
techniques.description: 先分配RW内存，写入后改为RX，避免RWX页面

techniques.name: NTDLL Unhooking
techniques.description: 从磁盘重新加载干净的NTDLL，移除EDR Hook

techniques.name: Shellcode Encryption (AES/RSA)
techniques.description: 使用AES或RSA加密shellcode，运行时解密

techniques.name: Process Injection
techniques.description: 注入shellcode到其他进程执行

techniques.name: Shellcode Encoding (Base64/UUID/IPv4)
techniques.description: 使用Base64、UUID或IPv4格式编码shellcode

techniques.name: Callback Execution
techniques.description: 通过Windows回调机制执行shellcode，避免显式线程创建

techniques.name: Sandbox Detection
techniques.description: 检测沙箱环境，避免在分析环境中执行

techniques.name: Anti-Debug
techniques.description: 检测调试器，防止动态分析

techniques.name: AMSI Bypass
techniques.description: 绕过反恶意软件扫描接口(AMSI)

techniques.name: ETW Bypass
techniques.description: 禁用ETW事件跟踪，避免遥测

techniques.name: Heap Allocation
techniques.description: 使用堆内存而非虚拟内存，降低检测率

techniques.name: Callstack Spoofing
techniques.description: 伪造调用栈，使敏感API调用看起来来自合法模块

techniques.name: Early Bird APC Injection
techniques.description: 在进程初始化前通过APC注入shellcode

techniques.name: Process Doppelganging
techniques.description: 使用NTFS事务创建进程，避免文件系统检测

techniques.name: Process Ghosting
techniques.description: 使用删除待定文件创建进程，更隐蔽的进程注入方式

techniques.name: PPID Spoofing
techniques.description: 伪造父进程ID，使进程看起来由合法进程启动

techniques.name: BlockDLLs Mitigation Policy
techniques.description: 阻止非微软DLL加载到进程，防止EDR注入

techniques.name: Thread Hijacking
techniques.description: 劫持现有线程执行shellcode，避免创建新线程

techniques.name: Sleep Obfuscation
techniques.description: 在睡眠时加密内存中的shellcode，避免内存扫描

techniques.name: Hell's Gate / TartarusGate
techniques.description: 动态从NTDLL解析系统调用号，绕过Hook

techniques.name: DLL Sideloading
techniques.description: 通过DLL劫持加载恶意DLL，利用合法程序执行

techniques.name: Anti-VM Detection
techniques.description: 检测虚拟机环境，避免在VM中执行

techniques.name: Shellcode Encryption (RC4/ChaCha20)
techniques.description: 使用RC4或ChaCha20等流密码加密shellcode

techniques.name: Fiber Execution
techniques.description: 通过Fiber机制执行shellcode，不创建线程

techniques.name: PEB Walking
techniques.description: 通过遍历PEB获取模块和API地址，避免使用GetModuleHandle和GetProcAddress

techniques.name: ACG (Arbitrary Code Guard)
techniques.description: 启用ACG策略防止动态代码修改

techniques.name: Reflective DLL Injection
techniques.description: 从内存中加载DLL，无需通过LoadLibrary，避免文件落地

techniques.name: COFF/BOF Loader
techniques.description: 在内存中加载和执行COFF文件和Cobalt Strike BOF，无需文件落地

techniques.name: Patchless AMSI Bypass
techniques.description: 使用硬件断点和VEH异常处理器绕过AMSI，无需修改内存中的AMSI代码

techniques.name: Alternative Callback Execution
techniques.description: 通过Windows各种回调函数执行shellcode，避免使用CreateThread等常见API

techniques.name: Donut PE Loader
techniques.description: 将.NET程序集、PE文件转换为位置无关的shellcode，从内存加载执行

techniques.name: Module Stomping
techniques.description: 将shellcode写入已加载模块的代码段，避免分配新的可执行内存区域

techniques.name: Named Pipe Impersonation
techniques.description: 通过命名管道模拟客户端身份，获取提升的权限

techniques.name: Section Mapping Injection
techniques.description: 使用内存映射文件在进程间共享shellcode，避免使用WriteProcessMemory

techniques.name: Position Independent Code (PIC)
techniques.description: 编写位置无关代码，避免使用全局变量和固定地址，可在任意内存位置执行

techniques.name: Indirect Clean Syscalls (HellHall)
techniques.description: 通过HellHall技术实现间接干净系统调用，从hooked函数中提取SSN并跳转到NTDLL中的syscall指令

techniques.name: FreshyCalls
techniques.description: 现代C++17风格的syscall库，无需太多样板代码即可舒适使用系统调用

techniques.name: Selective Syscall Unhooking
techniques.description: 选择性取消ntdll.dll的用户态hook，从已知干净的系统调用号重建系统调用

techniques.name: Transacted Hollowing
techniques.description: 进程镂空变种，结合NTFS事务和进程镂空技术，避免RWX内存权限

techniques.name: Polymorphic Shellcode
techniques.description: 变形引擎生成多态shellcode，每次编译产生不同的机器码但功能相同，绕过签名检测

techniques.name: ThreadPool Callstack Spoofing
techniques.description: 使用Windows线程池(Thread Pool)隐藏调用栈，结合间接系统调用避免EDR检测。通过tpWorker回调执行，使调用栈看起来来自ntdll.dll内部

techniques.name: Hell's Gate + Halo's Gate SSN Resolution
techniques.description: 动态从NTDLL解析系统调用号(SSN)，通过Hell's Gate和Halo's Gate技术绕过EDR Hook。当函数被Hook时(JMP指令)，通过遍历邻近系统调用计算目标SSN

techniques.name: AES-256-CTR / XChaCha20 Shellcode Encryption
techniques.description: 使用AES-256-CTR或XChaCha20等现代加密算法加密shellcode，提供更强的安全性

techniques.name: IAT Camouflage
techniques.description: 伪装导入地址表(IAT)，使其看起来像正常程序，隐藏真实使用的API

techniques.name: String Hashing (CRC32B/FNV1A32B/DJB2)
techniques.description: 使用CRC32B、FNV1A32B或DJB2等哈希算法隐藏字符串，避免静态分析

techniques.name: XLL/DLL Multi-Format Build
techniques.description: 加载器可编译为EXE、DLL或XLL格式，支持DLL侧加载攻击和多种部署方式

techniques.name: Remote Shellcode Download
techniques.description: 从远程服务器下载shellcode，避免静态检测。使用WinINet API下载payload到内存

techniques.name: Process Count Sandbox Check
techniques.description: 检查运行的进程数量，如果少于阈值(如100个)则可能为沙箱环境

techniques.name: ETW Patching
techniques.description: 禁用ETW事件跟踪，修补EtwEventWrite函数返回，防止遥测数据收集

techniques.name: Tickcount Sandbox Detection
techniques.description: 检查Sleep函数是否被沙箱操纵，通过比较实际睡眠时间与系统滴答计数来检测时间加速

techniques.name: Hard Disk Size Check
techniques.description: 检查硬盘大小，如果小于阈值(如100GB)则可能为虚拟机或沙箱环境

techniques.name: Self-Destruct / Melt
techniques.description: 执行后自删除，减少磁盘痕迹。可通过MoveFileEx标记删除或直接删除文件

techniques.name: Mutex Instance Check
techniques.description: 使用互斥体确保只有一个实例运行，避免多次执行被发现

techniques.name: Windows Defender Exclusion
techniques.description: 添加Windows Defender排除路径，需要管理员权限

techniques.name: Persistence via Scheduled Task
techniques.description: 通过计划任务实现持久化，在系统启动时自动执行

techniques.name: Deferred Execution (Defer)
techniques.description: 使用延迟执行技术绕过沙箱的时间限制，沙箱通常只运行程序短时间

techniques.name: Extended Callback Execution List
techniques.description: 扩展的回调执行方法列表，包括CertEnumSystemStore、CopyFile2、CreateTimerQueueTimer、CryptEnumOIDInfo、EnumCalendarInfo、EnumDisplayMonitors、EnumFontFamilies、FlsAlloc、ImageGetDigestStream等30+种回调方法

techniques.name: CRT Independence
techniques.description: 不依赖C运行时库(CRT)，减少导入和文件大小，提高隐蔽性

techniques.name: Commandline Argument Erasure
techniques.description: 清除命令行参数，避免分析工具查看执行参数。当通过rundll32执行时特别有用

techniques.name: Indirect Syscall via win32u.dll
techniques.description: 通过win32u.dll执行间接系统调用，比ntdll.dll更隐蔽，因为EDR更少监控win32u.dll

techniques.name: JMP Opcode Hook Detection
techniques.description: 检测函数是否被Hook，通过检查函数开头的JMP指令(E8/E9)来判断是否被EDR Hook

techniques.name: Threadless Inject + Module Stomping
techniques.description: 无线程注入技术结合模块覆盖，加载模块到目标进程并覆盖其代码段，然后恢复内存保护和原始状态

techniques.name: Hells Gate + Halos Gate SSN Resolution
techniques.description: Hell's Gate和Halo's Gate技术组合，动态从NTDLL解析系统调用号(SSN)。当函数被Hook时(JMP指令)，通过遍历邻近系统调用计算目标SSN，支持检测多种Hook位置(first, third, eighth, tenth, twelfth instruction)

techniques.name: Direct Syscall APC Injection
techniques.description: 使用直接系统调用进行APC注入，创建挂起进程后使用NtAllocateVirtualMemory、NtWriteVirtualMemory、NtProtectVirtualMemory系统调用分配和写入shellcode

techniques.name: API Hammering
techniques.description: 通过大量调用正常API来延迟执行，绕过沙箱的时间限制检测。使用大量无害API调用消耗时间，使沙箱超时或无法追踪完整执行流程

techniques.name: Self-Delete on Debug
techniques.description: 检测到调试器时自删除程序文件，移除磁盘痕迹并防止分析

techniques.name: Remote Mapping Injection
techniques.description: 使用NtCreateSection和NtMapViewOfSection在目标进程中映射shellcode，避免使用VirtualAllocEx和WriteProcessMemory等传统API

techniques.name: Brute-force Key Decryption
techniques.description: 使用暴力破解密钥解密payload，密钥可能从URL参数或其他动态来源获取，增加分析难度

techniques.name: EDR Preloader / AppVerifier Hijacking
techniques.description: 通过劫持AppVerifier层阻止EDR加载DLL或Hook进程。利用KiUserApcDispatcher hook在EDR之前运行，替换EDR入口点为无害函数，实现EDR模块失效

techniques.name: PoolParty - Thread Pool Injection
techniques.description: 利用Windows线程池(Thread Pool)进行进程注入，通过劫持线程池对象(Work, Wait, Timer, IO, ALPC, Job)实现代码执行，完全避免使用CreateThread/CreateRemoteThread等传统API

techniques.name: DLL Notification Callback Injection
techniques.description: 利用LdrpDllNotificationList注入shellcode。通过LdrRegisterDllNotification获取DLL通知列表头地址，在远程进程中插入伪造的通知条目，将回调函数指向shellcode

techniques.name: NullGate Indirect Syscall Library
techniques.description: 简化间接系统调用的C++库。自动从NTDLL解析系统调用号(SSN)，查找syscall指令地址，使用运行时XOR混淆函数名，提供类型安全的系统调用接口

techniques.name: Ntdll Unpatching from Known Syscall Numbers
techniques.description: 从已知的系统调用号重建被Hook的NTDLL函数。不依赖从磁盘读取NTDLL，而是使用已知的干净系统调用号直接执行系统调用

techniques.name: Debug API Injection
techniques.description: 使用Windows Debugging API实现无WriteProcessMemory的shellcode注入。通过DebugActiveProcess附加到目标进程，使用SetThreadContext修改线程上下文，利用硬件断点(HWBP)作为检查点，在目标进程上下文中执行代码。核心技巧是使用DbgBreakPoint获取返回地址，通过共享内存(FileMapping)传递数据

techniques.name: Badbyte Shellcode Sanitization
techniques.description: Shellcode坏字节清除工具，通过多种技术移除shellcode中的坏字节(如\x00)，同时保持功能不变。使用ML模型分析shellcode语义，生成等价但无坏字节的自修改代码

techniques.name: IPv6 Address Encoding
techniques.description: 将shellcode编码为IPv6地址字符串格式，在运行时解码执行。每个IPv6地址可编码16字节，使shellcode看起来像网络配置数据

techniques.name: AES-256-CBC Shellcode Encryption
techniques.description: 使用AES-256-CBC加密shellcode，配合Hell's Gate直接系统调用和Early Bird APC注入实现完整加载器链

techniques.name: Hardware Breakpoint Execution
techniques.description: 利用调试寄存器(DR0-DR3)设置硬件断点执行shellcode。通过设置DR7寄存器的相应位，可以在特定地址触发执行断点，无需修改代码段权限

techniques.name: IHxHelpPaneServer COM Execution
techniques.description: Cross-session COM object execution via IHxHelpPaneServer interface (CLSID: 8cec58ae-07a1-11d9-b15e-000d56bfe6ee) for arbitrary code execution. Uses IStandardActivator with ISpecialSystemProperties to set target session ID, then calls IHxHelpPaneServer::Execute with file:// URL to launch executables in other sessions without traditional process injection.

techniques.name: Thread Context ROP Gadget Injection
techniques.description: Process injection using SetThreadContext with push-push-ret ROP gadgets to redirect execution without CreateRemoteThread. Searches target process memory for unique PUSH REG1; PUSH REG2; RET gadgets, then uses SetThreadContext to set registers and RIP to execute arbitrary functions (VirtualAlloc, RtlFillMemory) before returning to shellcode. Avoids classic injection indicators.

techniques.name: NtCreateThread ROP Injection
techniques.description: Shellcode injection via NtCreateThread with custom stack allocation and ROP gadget execution. Allocates stack in target process, initializes INITIAL_TEB and CONTEXT structures, sets RIP to ROP gadget address. Uses NtCreateThread native API instead of CreateRemoteThread for lower detection surface. Combines with push-push-ret gadget to call arbitrary functions.

techniques.name: CraftUnhook Syscall Stub Crafting
techniques.description: Unhook NTDLL functions by crafting clean syscall stubs without reading from disk. Parses NTDLL exception directory (RUNTIME_FUNCTION table) to resolve system call numbers (SSN) by analyzing function order. Constructs fresh syscall stub template with proper WoW64 transition support. Overwrites hooked functions with clean stubs, then restores original bytes after call.

techniques.name: APC Queue Context Injection
techniques.description: Asynchronous Procedure Call injection using QueueUserAPC, QueueUserAPC2, NtQueueApcThread, NtQueueApcThreadEx, and NtQueueApcThreadEx2 with thread context manipulation. Targets alertable threads to queue shellcode execution without CreateRemoteThread. Can use special user APC flags (QUEUE_USER_APC_FLAGS_SPECIAL_USER_APC) for more reliable execution.

techniques.name: Ekko Timer Queue Sleep Obfuscation
techniques.description: Timer queue-based sleep obfuscation technique that uses CreateTimerQueueTimer and ROP chains via NtContinue to encrypt shellcode memory during sleep.

techniques.name: Cronos Waitable Timer Sleep Obfuscation
techniques.description: Waitable timer-based sleep obfuscation using CreateWaitableTimer and SetWaitableTimer with ROP chains. Creates multiple APC contexts for VirtualProtect, SystemFunction032 encryption, and sleep operations. Uses ROP gadgets (pop rcx; ret, pop rdx; ret, add rsp, 0x20; pop rdi; ret) for calling SleepEx with alertable=TRUE to trigger the queued APC callbacks. Encrypts PE image during sleep using AES-128 via SystemFunction032.

techniques.name: Shelter ROP-Based Sleep Obfuscation
techniques.description: ROP-based sleep obfuscation written in Rust that encrypts the entire PE or memory region during sleep using AES-128-CBC via BCrypt API. Uses ROP gadgets to call NtProtectVirtualMemory, BCryptEncrypt/Decrypt, and NtWaitForSingleObject without executing any code in the protected memory region. The stack is manipulated to execute a ROP chain that changes memory permissions, encrypts, waits, decrypts, and restores permissions.

techniques.name: HWSyscalls - Hardware Breakpoint Indirect Syscalls
techniques.description: Indirect syscall technique using hardware breakpoints (DR0-DR3) combined with HalosGate for SSN resolution. Sets hardware breakpoints at the syscall instruction and ret instruction addresses. Uses VEH exception handler to intercept execution, save context, and redirect to a synthetic trampoline in kernel32.dll. When the syscall is about to execute, HWBP triggers, the handler saves registers and redirects execution. When ret is hit, the original stack is restored, hiding the call stack.

techniques.name: Hardware Breakpoint Call Stack Spoofing
techniques.description: Call stack spoofing using hardware breakpoints to intercept both syscalls and API calls. Creates a fake call stack in heap memory with return addresses pointing to legitimate modules. Sets HWBP on target function addresses to intercept execution, swap to fake stack, execute the function, then restore original stack. Uses a canary value (0xDEADBEEFCAFEBABE) to locate stored context on stack. The fake stack is built from known stack frames in legitimate DLLs.

techniques.name: LayeredSyscall - VEH Call Stack Generation
techniques.description: Generates legitimate call stack frames using Vectored Exception Handling (VEH) combined with indirect syscalls. Uses HWBP to trigger exceptions when calling sensitive NT APIs, then builds a fake call stack in the exception handler that appears to come from legitimate modules (ntdll.dll, kernel32.dll). The synthetic call stack includes BaseThreadInitThunk and RtlUserThreadStart return addresses, making the call chain look legitimate to EDR stack inspection.

techniques.name: Fileless NTDLL Unhooking from Remote Server
techniques.description: Unhooks NTDLL by fetching a clean copy from a remote HTTP server instead of reading from disk. Avoids disk-based detection by downloading the clean NTDLL .text section from a C2 server, then patching the hooked NTDLL in memory. The remote server serves the clean .text section bytes which are then copied over the hooked NTDLL functions. Alternative sources include KnownDlls, suspended processes, and SSE (Shellcode Execution Environment).

techniques.name: SilentMoonwalk Dynamic Call Stack Spoofing
techniques.description: Fully dynamic call stack spoofing PoC that generates fake stack frames at runtime. Finds gadgets in legitimate modules to build return address chains. Uses a linked list of stack frames where each frame contains a return address and saved RBP. The fake stack is built to mimic a legitimate call chain, and execution is redirected via NtContinue with the spoofed context. Supports both x64 and x86 architectures.

techniques.name: Patchless AMSI/ETW Bypass via Hardware Breakpoint
techniques.description: Bypasses AMSI and ETW without patching memory by using hardware breakpoints and VEH exception handlers. Sets hardware breakpoints on AMSI/ETW functions (AmsiScanBuffer, EtwEventWrite) using debug registers DR0-DR3. When these functions are called, the VEH handler intercepts execution and modifies the return value to return AMSI_RESULT_CLEAN or STATUS_SUCCESS, then skips the actual function call. Leaves no memory modifications that can be detected by integrity checks.

techniques.name: Thread Stack Spoofing via Return Address Overwrite
techniques.description: Spoofs thread call stack by overwriting the return address on the stack during Sleep. When Sleep is called, the return address is saved and replaced with 0, effectively truncating the call stack from EDR memory scanners. After Sleep returns, the original return address is restored. This hides the shellcode memory allocation from call stack walking analysis. Hooks Sleep function to intercept and modify the stack.

techniques.name: Module Shifting Injection
techniques.description: Stealthier variation of Module Stomping that reduces memory IoCs. Instead of overwriting an entire module, it shifts the module's code section to make room for shellcode at the beginning, preserving some original module bytes. The original bytes are restored before module unloading. This avoids the telltale sign of having an entire module with completely overwritten code, while still hiding shellcode in a legitimate module's memory space.

techniques.name: syscalls-cpp Modern C++20 Syscall Library
techniques.description: Modern C++20 header-only library for advanced direct system call invocation. Provides type-safe syscall wrappers with compile-time SSN resolution. Supports both direct and indirect syscalls with automatic SSN extraction from NTDLL. Uses templates and concepts for clean API design. Handles WoW64 transitions automatically and provides syscall number caching for performance.

techniques.name: Gargoyle Memory Scanning Evasion
techniques.description: Memory scanning evasion technique that uses ROP gadgets and waitable timers to hide executable code when not in use. The PIC (Position Independent Code) marks itself as non-executable (PAGE_READWRITE) after execution and uses CreateWaitableTimer/SetWaitableTimer to schedule re-execution. Finds ROP gadgets (pop ecx; pop esp; ret or pop eax; pop esp; ret) in system DLLs like mshtml.dll to create a stack trampoline that flips memory permissions, executes payload, then flips back to RW and waits.

techniques.name: Phantom DLL Hollowing
techniques.description: Advanced DLL injection technique that maps a phantom DLL into target process memory without using LoadLibrary. Creates a phantom DLL image by mapping the DLL from disk, then hollowing out (overwriting) the legitimate DLL's code section with malicious payload while preserving the legitimate module's metadata. The phantom appears as a legitimate loaded module in the PEB, avoiding suspicious memory regions. Unlinks from PEB lists to hide from module enumeration.

techniques.name: DreamWalkers Reflective Loader with Stack Spoofing
techniques.description: Reflective shellcode loader with advanced call stack spoofing and .NET support. Generates fake call stacks at runtime by finding gadgets in legitimate modules. Supports both native shellcode and .NET assembly loading. The stack spoofing creates realistic call chains that pass through ntdll.dll, kernel32.dll, and other legitimate modules, making the execution appear normal to EDR stack inspection.

techniques.name: Unwinder Call Stack Spoofing for Rust
techniques.description: Rust implementation of call stack spoofing that mimics legitimate program behavior. Provides a Rust library for transparently spoofing call stacks when making syscalls or Windows API calls. Handles stack unwinding properly to maintain consistent fake call chains. Supports finding gadgets dynamically and building fake frames that pass through legitimate modules.

techniques.name: LLVM Call Obfuscator Plugin
techniques.description: LLVM compiler plugin that transparently applies stack spoofing and indirect syscalls to Windows x64 native calls at compile time. Automatically transforms all Windows API calls to use indirect syscalls with spoofed call stacks. No source code modifications required - the plugin handles everything at the IR level during compilation. Supports custom gadget finding and stack frame generation.
techniques.code_template:
# LLVM call obfuscator compilation
# Compile with the plugin to automatically apply stack spoofing
clang -Xclang -load -Xclang LLVMCallObfuscatorPass.so \
      -target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc \
      -DINDIRECT_SYSCALLS \
      -DSTACK_SPOOFING \
      source.c -o output.exe

# All Windows API calls are automatically transformed to:
# 1. Resolve SSN dynamically
# 2. Find syscall instruction address in ntdll
# 3. Build fake call stack
# 4. Execute indirect syscall with spoofed stack

techniques.name: VEH-based Syscall Invocation
techniques.description: Uses Vectored Exception Handling (VEH) to invoke syscalls without directly calling ntdll functions. Registers a VEH handler that intercepts INT 3 (breakpoint) exceptions. Sets up breakpoints at specific locations that trigger the VEH handler, which then performs the actual syscall. The call stack appears clean because the syscall happens in the exception handler context. Combines well with hardware breakpoints for additional evasion.

techniques.name: Early Cryo Bird Injection
techniques.description: APC injection using frozen Job Objects to create pre-frozen processes. Process is created in a frozen state via Job Object freeze information, allowing stealthier shellcode injection before process starts executing

techniques.name: EnumerateLoadedModules Callback Execution
techniques.description: Execute shellcode via EnumerateLoadedModules callback from Dbghelp.dll. Uses loaded module enumeration callback mechanism to trigger code execution without explicit thread creation

techniques.name: ReadProcessMemory Indirect Write
techniques.description: Exploit ReadProcessMemory lpNumberOfBytesRead parameter to write shellcode indirectly. The out pointer parameter enables writing to process memory without using common APIs like memcpy or WriteProcessMemory

techniques.name: Threadless Injection
techniques.description: Inject shellcode into existing process without creating new thread by manipulating existing thread context or using existing callbacks. Avoids creating new threads which is heavily monitored by EDR

techniques.name: Drip Loader
techniques.description: Evade event-based injection detection by delaying memory permission changes and splitting shellcode injection across multiple operations. Separates RW and RX permission phases to bypass event tracing detection

techniques.name: Process Hollowing
techniques.description: Create suspended process, unmap its memory (NtUnmapViewOfSection), allocate memory at same base address, write shellcode/DLL, set thread context to entry point, and resume. More specific than generic process injection.

techniques.name: sRDI Shellcode Conversion
techniques.description: Convert DLL to position-independent shellcode using sRDI (Shellcode Reflective DLL Injection). The converted shellcode can be injected like regular shellcode without needing a PE loader

techniques.name: NtQueueApcThreadEx Local Execution
techniques.description: Queue APC to current process thread using NtQueueApcThreadEx native API for local shellcode execution. Similar to QueueUserAPC but uses direct syscall

techniques.name: NINA - No Injection No Allocation
techniques.description: Process injection technique that executes shellcode in remote processes without using VirtualAllocEx or CreateRemoteThread. Uses SetThreadContext to manipulate thread execution, finds existing RW memory pages in target process for stack data, finds existing RX pages for shellcode placement in the executable image itself (avoiding PE-sieve detection), and uses ReadProcessMemory/WriteProcessMemory via the target process itself to transfer data. Requires process handle duplication to allow target to read attacker process memory.

techniques.name: Handle Hijacking for Thread Pool Objects
techniques.description: Technique to hijack handles to thread pool objects (TpWorkerFactory, IoCompletion, IRTimer) from a target process. Uses NtQueryInformationProcess with ProcessHandleInformation to enumerate all handles in the target process, then NtQueryObject to identify the object type, and DuplicateHandle to steal handles with desired access rights. Enables subsequent PoolParty injection variants.

techniques.name: Worker Factory Start Routine Overwrite
techniques.description: PoolParty variant that overwrites the StartRoutine of a worker factory to execute shellcode. Hijacks the TpWorkerFactory handle from the target process, queries its WORKER_FACTORY_BASIC_INFORMATION to get the StartRoutine address, and uses NtSetInformationWorkerFactory to trigger execution by increasing the minimum thread count. No VirtualAllocEx needed - reuses existing code pages.

techniques.name: Remote TP Work Insertion
techniques.description: PoolParty variant that creates a malicious TP_WORK structure in the local process, modifies it to point to the target process TP_POOL, and writes it to the target process. The work item is inserted into the high-priority task queue of the target process thread pool, causing the shellcode callback to execute when the thread pool processes the work item.

techniques.name: Remote TP Timer Insertion
techniques.description: PoolParty variant that crafts a malicious TP_TIMER structure pointing to shellcode and inserts it into the target process timer queue. Hijacks both TpWorkerFactory and IRTimer handles, allocates the timer structure in target process, and uses NtSetTimer2 to trigger TppTimerQueueExpiration which executes the callback.

techniques.name: Remote TP Wait Insertion via IO Completion
techniques.description: PoolParty variant that creates a TP_WAIT structure, allocates it in target process, and associates it with the hijacked IO completion port via ZwAssociateWaitCompletionPacket. Sets an event to queue a packet to the IO completion port, triggering the wait callback in the target process.

techniques.name: Remote TP IO Insertion
techniques.description: PoolParty variant that creates a TP_IO structure associated with a file handle and the target process IO completion port. Performs asynchronous file write which queues a completion packet to the IOCP, causing the shellcode callback to execute in the target process context.

techniques.name: Remote TP ALPC Insertion
techniques.description: PoolParty variant that uses ALPC (Advanced Local Procedure Call) port to trigger shellcode execution. Creates TP_ALPC structure, associates it with an ALPC port connected to the target process IO completion port, and sends a message to trigger the callback.

techniques.name: Remote TP Job Insertion
techniques.description: PoolParty variant that uses job objects to trigger shellcode. Creates TP_JOB structure with shellcode callback, associates the job object with the target process IO completion port, and assigns the current process to the job to trigger the notification callback in target process.

techniques.name: Remote TP Direct Insertion
techniques.description: PoolParty variant that directly queues a packet to the hijacked IO completion port. Creates a TP_DIRECT structure with the shellcode callback address and uses ZwSetIoCompletion to queue it directly to the target process worker factory IO completion port.

techniques.name: EtwpCreateEtwThread Execution
techniques.description: Shellcode execution using the undocumented EtwpCreateEtwThread function from ntdll. Allocates RW memory, copies shellcode, changes protection to RX, and creates a new thread through the ETW (Event Tracing for Windows) subsystem. Avoids the standard CreateThread/CreateRemoteThread APIs.

techniques.name: NtQueueApcThreadEx Special User APC
techniques.description: Shellcode execution using NtQueueApcThreadEx with QUEUE_USER_APC_FLAGS_SPECIAL_USER_APC flag. This allows APC injection without alertable thread state requirement. Creates a special user-mode APC that executes immediately on the current thread.

techniques.name: Peruns Unhooking
techniques.description: Unhooking technique that spawns a suspended clean process (e.g., notepad.exe), reads the unhooked ntdll.dll .text section from it, and overwrites the hooked ntdll in the current process. Bypasses EDR hooks by using a clean copy from a fresh process.

techniques.name: Phant0m Event Log Termination
techniques.description: Technique to disable Windows Event Log service by terminating all threads belonging to EventLog service process. Enumerates all threads, identifies those belonging to EventLog via service tag query (I_QueryTagInformation), and terminates them. Effectively stops event logging.

techniques.name: Entry Point Overwrite No RWX
techniques.description: Process hollowing variant that avoids RWX memory. Creates a suspended process, reads the entry point from the PE header, overwrites the entry point with shellcode, and resumes the thread. No VirtualAllocEx or RWX pages needed.

techniques.name: BYOVD - Driver Loading via SCM
techniques.description: Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver technique: Load a legitimately signed but vulnerable kernel driver using Windows Service Control Manager API. The driver is dropped to disk, registered as a kernel service, and started to gain kernel-level execution. This technique bypasses security software by operating at Ring 0. Common vulnerable drivers include RTCore64.sys, DBUtil_2_3.sys, gdrv.sys, and Process Hacker driver. Requires administrator privileges.

techniques.name: BYOVD - Kernel Memory Read/Write Primitives
techniques.description: BYOVD technique to achieve arbitrary kernel memory read/write operations through vulnerable driver IOCTLs. Common drivers like RTCore64.sys provide IOCTLs (0x80002048 for read, 0x8000204C for write) that allow reading/writing physical memory addresses. This enables: 1) Disabling DSE (Driver Signature Enforcement) by modifying g_CiOptions in CI.dll, 2) Bypassing PatchGuard by modifying callback pointers, 3) Modifying EPROCESS structures for token manipulation, 4) Disabling ETW/AMSI kernel callbacks.

techniques.name: BYOVD - Process Termination via IOCTL
techniques.description: BYOVD technique for terminating protected processes (AV/EDR/PPL) using vulnerable kernel drivers. The attacker loads a driver that exposes IOCTL interfaces for ZwTerminateProcess. Notable drivers and IOCTLs: gmer.sys (Blackout) with IOCTLs 0x9876C004 and 0x9876C094, Process Hacker driver with various process termination IOCTLs, TfSysMon.sys with IOCTL 0xB4A00404. Can bypass PPL (Protected Process Light) protections and terminate normally unkillable security processes.

techniques.name: BYOVD - DSE Bypass
techniques.description: Bypass Driver Signature Enforcement (DSE) using vulnerable drivers. By leveraging kernel memory write primitives from BYOVD, attackers can modify g_CiOptions in CI.dll to disable code integrity checks. This allows loading unsigned malicious drivers. The technique involves: 1) Finding ntoskrnl base, 2) Locating SeValidateImageHeader/SeValidateImageData callbacks, 3) Patching them to return success, 4) Loading unsigned driver. Works on Windows 10/11 but may trigger PatchGuard. Referenced in dse_pg_bypass project.

techniques.name: BYOVD - LSA Protection Bypass
techniques.description: Disable LSA Protection (RunAsPPL) using vulnerable drivers to enable LSASS memory dumping. Attackers use kernel memory write capabilities to modify the EPROCESS structure of LSASS: changing SignatureLevel, SectionSignatureLevel, and Type fields to remove PPL protection. Common drivers: DBUtil_2_3.sys (Dell), RTCore64.sys (MSI Afterburner), and gdrv.sys (Gigabyte). After bypassing LSA protection, tools like Mimikatz can dump credentials from LSASS memory. Implemented in tools like Dellicious and PPLKiller.

techniques.name: BYOVD - Token Manipulation
techniques.description: Privilege escalation via token manipulation using vulnerable drivers. Attackers use kernel memory read/write to copy a privileged token (typically SYSTEM) to a target process. The technique involves: 1) Finding EPROCESS of SYSTEM process, 2) Reading token value from EPROCESS+ProcessToken offset, 3) Writing token to target process EPROCESS. Alternatively, can modify Privilege array in TOKEN structure to enable SeDebugPrivilege, SeLoadDriverPrivilege, etc. BYOVDKit provides token copy functionality using DBUtil_2_3.sys and RTCore64.sys.

techniques.name: BYOVD - ETW/TI Provider Disable
techniques.description: Disable ETW Threat Intelligence provider using vulnerable drivers to evade kernel-level telemetry. The technique uses kernel memory write to modify EtwThreatIntProvRegHandle global variable in ntoskrnl, preventing the ETW TI provider from receiving security events. This disables kernel telemetry for EDR products that rely on ETW. The offset is found by analyzing KeInsertQueueApc function. Implementation: calculate offset dynamically by parsing ntoskrnl, find EtwThreatIntProvRegHandle, write zero to disable the provider.

techniques.name: BYOVD - HVCI Bypass via Vulnerable Driver
techniques.description: Exploit vulnerable drivers that bypass Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity (HVCI). Some legitimate signed drivers expose functionality that can be weaponized even with HVCI enabled. These drivers include: gmer.sys (Blackout), certain AV drivers (BdApiUtil64.sys, K7RKScan.sys, ksapi64.sys), and virtualization-based drivers. The key is finding drivers that are not blocked by HVCI policy. BYOVDFinder and HolyGrail tools help identify LOLDrivers not blocked by active HVCI policy.

techniques.name: BYOVD - Kernel Callback Removal
techniques.description: Remove kernel security callbacks (ObRegisterCallbacks, PsSetCreateProcessNotifyRoutine, etc.) using vulnerable drivers. EDR products register kernel callbacks to monitor process creation, thread creation, image loading, and object access. BYOVD can disable these callbacks by: 1) Finding callback array in kernel memory, 2) Overwriting callback function pointers, 3) Or modifying PspCreateProcessNotifyRoutine/PspCreateThreadNotifyRoutine arrays. RTCore_read_write_primitive project demonstrates removing kernel callbacks to blind EDR.

techniques.name: BYOVD - Network Connection Blocking
techniques.description: Use vulnerable drivers with WFP (Windows Filtering Platform) capabilities to block EDR/XDR network connections. Some drivers like 360WFP.sys expose WFP functionality that can be abused to create callout filters blocking outbound connections from specific processes. This prevents EDR agents from communicating with their cloud backend for telemetry upload and command/response. Attackers can selectively block security product network traffic while maintaining connectivity for malicious operations.

techniques.name: BYOVD - File System Access Bypass
techniques.description: Use vulnerable drivers to access protected files bypassing NTFS permissions and file locks. BYOVDKit demonstrates copying protected files like SAM registry hive and NTDS.dit (Active Directory database) using kernel-level file operations. The driver operates at kernel level where DACL checks can be bypassed. Technique involves: 1) Opening file handle via kernel driver, 2) Reading file contents via IOCTL, 3) Writing to attacker-controlled location. Useful for credential harvesting from protected system files.

techniques.name: Vulnerable Driver - RTCore64.sys
techniques.description: MSI Afterburner's RTCore64.sys driver is one of the most abused vulnerable drivers for BYOVD attacks. Exposes IOCTLs for arbitrary physical memory read (0x80002048) and write (0x8000204C). Can be used for: kernel memory manipulation, DSE bypass, process token theft, and disabling security features. Legitimately signed by MSI but weaponized by attackers. Listed in LOLDrivers and Microsoft's driver block list.

techniques.name: Vulnerable Driver - Process Hacker (kprocesshacker.sys)
techniques.description: Process Hacker driver (kprocesshacker.sys) provides extensive kernel manipulation capabilities including process termination, memory operations, and handle manipulation. Exposes numerous IOCTLs documented in Process Hacker source code. Requires SeDebugPrivilege but can terminate PPL processes. Used in Overlord project for process termination. The driver is legitimately signed but its powerful IOCTLs can be abused. IOCTL codes documented in kphapi.h header.

techniques.name: Driver Reverse Engineering for BYOVD
techniques.description: Methodology for discovering vulnerable drivers suitable for BYOVD attacks: 1) Check driver imports for ZwOpenProcess AND ZwTerminateProcess (potential process killer), 2) Locate DriverEntry and trace IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL handler, 3) Identify IOCTL codes and input buffer structures, 4) Analyze security checks (or lack thereof), 5) Map complete attack chain from user IOCTL to dangerous kernel operations. Tools like HolyGrail help prioritize drivers worth manual analysis. BlackSnufkin's BYOVD repository documents complete reverse engineering methodology.

techniques.name: BYOVD - PatchGuard Bypass
techniques.description: Bypass Kernel Patch Protection (PatchGuard) using vulnerable drivers. PatchGuard detects kernel modifications but has a delay before triggering BSOD. Techniques include: 1) Finding PatchGuard context in nt!KiFilterFiberContext, 2) Modifying callback pointers that PatchGuard monitors, 3) Exploiting the initialization delay. The dse_pg_bypass project demonstrates disabling both DSE and PatchGuard by writing specific values to kernel memory. Note: This is highly unstable and system-specific.

techniques.name: LOLDrivers Database Utilization
techniques.description: LOLDrivers (Living Off The Land Drivers) is a database of known vulnerable and malicious drivers. BYOVD attackers use this resource to find legitimately signed drivers with exploitable IOCTLs. The database includes: driver hashes, signatures, IOCTL codes, capabilities (read/write/terminate), and Microsoft block status. Tools like BYOVDFinder identify which LOLDrivers are not blocked by HVCI policy. HolyGrail helps prioritize drivers for analysis. Reference: https://www.loldrivers.io
